pattern: Every position you flip on can only ever glue together at most the two runs of ones sitting directly beside it, so instead of rescanning the whole string at each step you can track, incrementally, how the length of the run at each end changes and how many runs currently have exactly the length you care about.
hints: Think about what changes when a single new position turns from 0 to 1: nothing happens anywhere except right where it merges with whatever run of ones was already touching its left neighbor and whatever run was touching its right neighbor.

Keep an array that records, for the two endpoints of every run of ones, how long that run currently is, and keep a running tally of how many runs exist for each possible length; then a single flip only needs to remove the two old run lengths from the tally (if they existed) and add the one new merged length.

The step you are looking for is simply the last step at which the tally for your target length m is still greater than zero -- once it drops to zero for good it never reappears, since merging never shrinks the run around a newly-set position, so track the max step while that tally count stays positive rather than only checking the very end.

edge_inputs.description: target length equal to the whole array, filled left to right
target length equal to the whole array, filled right to left
interleaved fill order that repeatedly merges and re-splits runs
a different merge pattern where the target-length runs vanish partway through
a longer ascending fill so the target group only ever exists at one exact step

stress.description: random permutation fill order with a random target length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    arr = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    m = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [arr, m]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def findLatestStep(self, arr, m):
        n = len(arr)
        length = [0] * (n + 2)
        cnt = [0] * (n + 1)
        ans = -1
        for step, pos in enumerate(arr, start=1):
            left = length[pos - 1]
            right = length[pos + 1]
            new_len = left + right + 1
            length[pos] = new_len
            length[pos - left] = new_len
            length[pos + right] = new_len
            if left > 0:
                cnt[left] -= 1
            if right > 0:
                cnt[right] -= 1
            cnt[new_len] += 1
            if cnt[m] > 0:
                ans = step
        return ans
